posts.author: Shail1234
posts.community: Veefin Solutions - A fintech SME with large ambitions!
posts.text: Yes, positive for sure. They were working on it genuinely by doing quarterly results and other formalities needed. Veefin listed on the stock market on July 5, 2023 . So 3 years by next month. Shareholder count is around 5000, minimum needed is 500 so no issues, minimum equity capital needed is 10cr. They will go through. Efforts are visible. Next month concall would be super interesting. Migration + merger + numbers of PSB as Mr Raja said fy27 is execution time, so market will watch eagerly because so far price action is mediocre. Price is not strong. Market is waiting for numbers.

posts.author: UdayChandak
posts.community: Veefin Solutions - A fintech SME with large ambitions!
posts.text: IMG_2933 1170×1464 231 KB Positive hopefully Migration from BSE SME to BSE Main Board Direct listing on NSE Main Board Postal ballot approval from shareholders for the above Other business Veefin seems broadly eligible on size, capital, market cap and profitability. The remaining watch items are three-year listing timing, public shareholder count, exchange due diligence, compliance record, and final BSE/NSE approval.

posts.author: Shail1234
posts.community: Veefin Solutions - A fintech SME with large ambitions!
posts.text: Big appointment of Riddhi Dutta as Chief Revenue Officer. The guy spent 7 years in Backbase as VP Asia, (after starting from base) a company with roughly ₹2,500–3,000 crore annual revenue and ₹22,000+ crore valuation. He is joining a 750 cr valuation company Veefin. Hopefully make it reach 22000 cr in 7 years as well.

posts.author: UdayChandak
posts.community: Veefin Solutions - A fintech SME with large ambitions!
posts.text: Fair question. As outside investors, I don’t think we can track every internal operating number of PSB Xchange in real time. The clean sources for us are PSB Alliance updates, PSB Xchange public information, Veefin investor presentations, concalls, bank/anchor announcements, and eventually reported revenue. But we also need to remember that this is a platform business. Not every operating detail will be visible from outside, and not every small movement will be disclosed immediately. So I would look at it this way: we should track whatever is publicly available, ask management for better discl…

posts.author: Shail1234
posts.community: Veefin Solutions - A fintech SME with large ambitions!
posts.text: Uday ji any way to track as you mentioned numbers that have already entered the operating layer — signed, integrated, live, active programs, active channel partners, utilisation/ADPO, and actual billing ? If only via investor presetation or concall ?

posts.author: UdayChandak
posts.community: Veefin Solutions - A fintech SME with large ambitions!
posts.text: On the first point — why RR Kabel or any corporate has to come through a fintech instead of directly going to PSB Alliance — my understanding is slightly different. It is not that RR Kabel cannot talk to a public sector bank directly. Of course it can. Large corporates already have banking relationships. But PSB Xchange is not just a loan enquiry website. It is an operating workflow where anchor, dealer/vendor, sourcing partner, platform, and lender all interact in a structured way. That is where the fintech/sourcing partner comes in. Think of a dealer sitting in a Tier-2 or Tier-3 market. He…

posts.author: UdayChandak
posts.community: Veefin Solutions - A fintech SME with large ambitions!
posts.text: A positive corporate testimonial would be the cleanest soft trigger. One real acknowledgement from a serious corporate is more powerful than 20 presentation slides , because it proves the platform is solving a real operating problem, not just creating a nice investor narrative. If one anchor says PSB Xchange made dealer/vendor financing faster, smoother, or more efficient, it can create a ripple effect. Other corporates trust operating proof more than platform claims. But even if no corporate publicly acknowledges it, that does not automatically weaken the thesis. Many corporates may not want…

posts.text: @shubham2 Thank you Shubham ji. Yes for purpose of scaling and swift adoption they are using fintech for enroling companies. But they are open to direct enrolment in future as per their IR.

posts.author: Shubham2
posts.community: Veefin Solutions - A fintech SME with large ambitions!
posts.text: @Shail1234 This is my understanding of PSB exchange. Onboarding an Enterprise is a tedious task. I believe Veefin just wants to focus on the technology part and doesn’t want the hassles of onboarding big companies/conglomerates when they can just share few margin points with a fintech.
